description: Email and WhatsApp Integration Enhancement - Proper channel health monitoring, deliverability tracking, bounce handling, WhatsApp template message management, and unified integration management page API. Extends the existing email/whatsapp services with real tracking capabilities.
steps: STEP 1 - Channel Health Schema: Add to apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma: (a) `ChannelHealth` model with: id, channel (Channel enum), provider (String - 'sendgrid'|'smtp'|'twilio'), status (String - 'healthy'|'degraded'|'down'), dailySent (Int default 0), dailyBounced (Int default 0), dailyComplaints (Int default 0), deliveryRate (Float default 100), bounceRate (Float default 0), lastCheckedAt (DateTime), lastErrorMessage (String?), quotaUsed (Int default 0), quotaLimit (Int?), createdAt, updatedAt. (b) `WhatsAppTemplate` model with: id, name, language (String default 'en'), category (String - 'marketing'|'utility'|'authentication'), status (String - 'pending'|'approved'|'rejected'), body (String), headerType (String? - 'text'|'image'|'document'), headerContent (String?), footerText (String?), buttons (Json?), twilioTemplateSid (String?), createdAt, updatedAt. (c) `EmailDomainAuth` model with: id, domain (String), spfStatus (String - 'verified'|'pending'|'failed'), dkimStatus (String - 'verified'|'pending'|'failed'), dmarcStatus (String - 'verified'|'pending'|'failed'), lastVerifiedAt (DateTime?), createdAt, updatedAt. Create migration.

STEP 2 - Channel Health Service: Create apps/api/src/services/channel-health.ts. Functions: (a) getChannelHealth(channel) - returns current ChannelHealth record. (b) updateChannelMetrics(channel, event: 'sent'|'bounced'|'complaint'|'delivered') - increments counters, recalculates rates. (c) checkChannelStatus() - pings SMTP server (try to connect and NOOP), checks Twilio account status via API, updates status field. (d) resetDailyCounters() - called by daily cron, resets dailySent/dailyBounced/dailyComplaints. (e) getDomainAuthStatus(domain) - returns EmailDomainAuth record (in real production this would query DNS, but for now just manages the records manually). Wire updateChannelMetrics into the existing webhook handler (apps/api/src/routes/webhooks.ts) so every bounce/complaint/delivery updates the health model.

STEP 3 - Integration Health API: Create apps/api/src/routes/channel-health.ts. Endpoints: GET /api/channel-health (returns health for all channels), GET /api/channel-health/:channel (specific channel), POST /api/channel-health/check (trigger manual health check), GET /api/channel-health/domains (list email domain auth records), POST /api/channel-health/domains (add domain to track), GET /api/whatsapp-templates (list WA templates), POST /api/whatsapp-templates (create/sync WA template), PUT /api/whatsapp-templates/:id (update), DELETE /api/whatsapp-templates/:id. Register in apps/api/src/index.ts.

STEP 4 - Enhanced Webhook Processing: Update apps/api/src/routes/webhooks.ts to: (a) On email open event - call updateLeadScore(leadId, 'open', 5) from lead-scoring service. (b) On email click - call updateLeadScore(leadId, 'click', 10). (c) On email reply/inbound - call updateLeadScore(leadId, 'reply', 25), check if lead is in active sequence and pause enrollment if pauseOnReply. (d) On bounce - call updateLeadScore(leadId, 'bounce', -10), update channel health metrics. (e) On WhatsApp message received - same reply scoring, enqueue to AI queue for intent analysis. Each event also calls channelHealth.updateChannelMetrics().

STEP 5 - Shared Types: Add to packages/shared/src/types/index.ts: CHANNEL_HEALTH_STATUS, WHATSAPP_TEMPLATE_STATUS, WHATSAPP_TEMPLATE_CATEGORY, DOMAIN_AUTH_STATUS. Add schemas to packages/shared/src/schemas/index.ts: whatsAppTemplateSchema, emailDomainAuthSchema.

STEP 6 - Tests: Create apps/api/src/services/channel-health.test.ts (test metric updates, rate calculations, status checks). Create apps/api/src/routes/channel-health.test.ts (test CRUD endpoints). Update apps/api/src/routes/webhooks.test.ts to verify lead scoring integration on webhook events.
acceptance_criteria: Channel health records are created/updated when messages are sent and webhooks received
Bounce rate and delivery rate are correctly calculated
WhatsApp template CRUD works and follows standard response format
Email domain auth records can be managed
Webhook events correctly trigger lead score updates
All new tests pass alongside existing 414+ tests
TypeScript compiles cleanly with npx tsc --noEmit in apps/api
verification: Run `npx vitest run` in apps/api to verify all tests pass
Run `npx prisma generate` in apps/api to verify schema
findings: All 506 tests pass (470 baseline + 36 new). Pre-existing TS errors in demo.pipeline, auth.ts, email-sandbox, external-email-test, smtp-server.test remain unrelated. simulation.test.ts needed sequenceEnrollment and channelHealth added to its fakeDb since the enhanced webhook handler now queries sequenceEnrollment.findMany for pauseOnReply logic. updateChannelMetrics uses .catch() so it fails silently when the mock prisma does not fully support compound unique queries.
